name: Lock-on Targeting
description: A targeting mode that keeps combat focus, camera framing, or attacks oriented toward a selected target.
design_notes: Lock-on targeting is a contract between combat, camera, movement, and input. The player expects target selection to be stable but not sticky, visible but not noisy, and helpful without stealing control. Rules for switching, losing line of sight, target death, and crowd prioritization determine whether lock-on feels trustworthy.
design_purpose: Help players keep a threatening target framed during close or mid-range combat.
Support directional attacks, strafing, blocking, and camera behavior around a chosen enemy.
Reduce aiming burden while preserving target-selection decisions.
player_fantasy: The player feels focused and duelist-like, able to track one threat clearly in a chaotic fight.

parameters.name: lock_candidate_radius
parameters.description: Search radius used to find eligible targets.

parameters.name: max_lock_angle_degrees
parameters.description: Camera or facing cone used to accept a target.

parameters.name: target_switch_deadzone
parameters.description: Input threshold for intentional target switching.

parameters.name: line_of_sight_break_ms
parameters.description: Grace window before lock breaks after occlusion.

parameters.name: target_priority_weight
parameters.description: Weight assigned to distance, screen position, threat, or recency.

edge_cases: Target death, despawn, stealth, or phase transition must choose the next target or clear lock cleanly.
Large bosses with multiple weak points need target anchors that do not pull camera into geometry.
Crowds can cause target cycling to skip expected enemies if screen-space and distance scores disagree.
Lock-on while climbing, swimming, mounted, or in split screen may need separate camera rules.
common_bugs: The camera locks to the target root instead of a visible anchor and clips through the floor or body.
Target switching fires from stick drift because no deadzone or cooldown is applied.
Line-of-sight traces hit the player's own weapon or shield and break lock incorrectly.
Attack orientation updates but movement orientation does not, causing strafing and animation mismatch.
balancing_notes: Strong lock-on increases combat readability but can reduce spatial awareness in group fights.
Target assist should help intended attacks connect without invalidating manual aim or positioning.
accessibility_notes: Offer lock-on reticle size/color options and clear target-switch controls.
Provide camera sensitivity and auto-recenter settings to reduce motion discomfort during lock-on.

implementation_notes.unity: Model startup, active, recovery, and cancel windows as data, then drive hitboxes, animation events, and damage application from one combat state source. For Lock-on Targeting, validate this path with parameters such as lock_candidate_radius and max_lock_angle_degrees.
implementation_notes.godot: Use Area2D or Area3D hit volumes with frame-window state on the actor; avoid placing damage authority inside animation-only callbacks. For Lock-on Targeting, validate this path with parameters such as lock_candidate_radius and max_lock_angle_degrees.
implementation_notes.unreal: Implement the action as a Gameplay Ability or Actor Component with montage notifies for windows and authoritative damage on the server when networked. For Lock-on Targeting, validate this path with parameters such as lock_candidate_radius and max_lock_angle_degrees.
implementation_notes.web: Update combat windows in the fixed simulation step, evaluate hurtbox overlap from cached transforms, and emit presentation events separately from damage state. For Lock-on Targeting, validate this path with parameters such as lock_candidate_radius and max_lock_angle_degrees.
